final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Secure AWS Cognito User Management System with Admin-Managed Provisioning, Custom Password Policy, Hosted UI Domain, and OAuth 2.0 Configuration'

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Environment name (e.g., dev, staging, prod)'
    Default: dev
  ApplicationName:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Application name for resource naming and tagging'
    Default: cognito-app
  Owner:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Owner or team responsible for the resource'
    Default: platform-team
  CloudFrontDistributionArn:
    Type: String
    Description: 'ARN of the CloudFront distribution for Cognito Hosted UI domain'
    Default: ''

Resources:
  CognitoUserPool:
    Type: AWS::Cognito::UserPool
    Properties:
      UserPoolName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-user-pool-${Environment}'
      AutoVerifiedAttributes:
        - email
      EmailVerificationSubject: 'Verify your email for Application'
      EmailVerificationMessage: 'Your verification code is {####}. Valid for 72 hours.'
      UsernameAttributes:
        - email
      AccountRecoverySetting:
        RecoveryMechanisms:
          - Priority: 1
            Name: verified_email
      MfaConfiguration: 'OFF'
      SmsConfiguration:
        ExternalId: !Ref 'AWS::AccountId'
      Schema:
        - Name: email
          AttributeDataType: String
          Mutable: true
          Required: true
        - Name: name
          AttributeDataType: String
          Mutable: true
          Required: false
      DeviceConfiguration:
        DeviceOnlyRememberedOnUserPrompt: true
      LambdaConfig:
        PreAuthentication: !GetAtt CognitoPreAuthLambda.Arn
        PostAuthentication: !GetAtt CognitoPostAuthLambda.Arn
        CustomMessage: !GetAtt CognitoCustomMessageLambda.Arn

  CognitoUserPoolDomain:
    Type: AWS::Cognito::UserPoolDomain
    Properties:
      Domain: !Ref CloudFrontDistributionArn
      UserPoolId: !Ref CognitoUserPool

  CognitoUserPoolClient:
    Type: AWS::Cognito::UserPoolClient
    Properties:
      ClientName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-client-${Environment}'
      UserPoolId: !Ref CognitoUserPool
      GenerateSecret: true
      ExplicitAuthFlows:
        - ADMIN_NO_SRP_AUTH
        - REFRESH_TOKEN_AUTH
        - REFRESH_TOKEN
        - CUSTOM_AUTH
        - USER_PASSWORD_AUTH
      AllowedOAuthFlows:
        - code
        - implicit
      AllowedOAuthScopes:
        - openid
        - email
        - profile
        - aws.cognito.signin.user.admin
      AllowedOAuthFlowsUserPoolClient: true
      CallbackURLs:
        - !Sub 'https://${CloudFrontDistributionArn}/callback'
      LogoutURLs:
        - !Sub 'https://${CloudFrontDistributionArn}/logout'
      DefaultRedirectURI: !Sub 'https://${CloudFrontDistributionArn}/callback'
      SupportedIdentityProviders:
        - COGNITO
      PreventUserExistenceErrors: 'ENABLED'
      EnableTokenRevocation: true
      AccessTokenValidity: 1
      IdTokenValidity: 1
      RefreshTokenValidity: 30
      TokenValidityUnits:
        AccessToken: minutes
        IdToken: minutes
        RefreshToken: days
      ReadAttributes:
        - email
        - name
        - profile
      WriteAttributes:
        - email
        - name

  CognitoUserPoolIdentityProvider:
    Type: AWS::Cognito::UserPoolIdentityProvider
    Properties:
      ProviderName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-idp-${Environment}'
      ProviderType: SAML
      UserPoolId: !Ref CognitoUserPool
      ProviderDetails:
        MetadataURL: !Sub 'https://${CloudFrontDistributionArn}/saml/metadata'
        IDPIdentifier: !Ref CloudFrontDistributionArn
        AttributeMapping:
          email=email
          name=name

  CognitoUserPoolClientIAMRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-cognito-client-role-${Environment}'
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - lambda.amazonaws.com
                - cognito-identity.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-cognito-client-policy-${Environment}'
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - cognito-idp:AdminCreateUser
                  - cognito-idp:AdminSetUserPassword
                  - cognito-idp:AdminSetUserSettings
                  - cognito-idp:AdminUpdateUserAttributes
                  - cognito-idp:AdminDisableUser
                  - cognito-idp:AdminEnableUser
                  - cognito-idp:AdminInitiateAuth
                  - cognito-idp:AdminRespondToAuthChallenge
                  - cognito-idp:GlobalSignOut
                  - cognito-idp:ForgotPassword
                  - cognito-idp:ConfirmSignUp
                  - cognito-idp:ChangePassword
                  - cognito-idp:InitiateAuth
                  - cognito-idp:RespondToAuthChallenge
                  - cognito-identity:GetCredentialsForIdentity
                  - cognito-identity:GetId
                  - cognito-identity:UnlinkIdentity
                  - cognito-identity:DescribeIdentity
                  - cognito-identity:ListIdentities
                  - cognito-identity:CreateIdentityPool
                  - cognito-identity:UpdateIdentityPool
                Resource: '*'

  CognitoPreAuthLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-preauth-${Environment}'
      Handler: index.handler
      Role: !GetAtt CognitoPreAuthLambdaRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          def handler(event, context):
              return event
      Runtime: python3.12
      Timeout: 3
      MemorySize: 128
      Environment:
        Variables:
          ENVIRONMENT: !Ref Environment
          APPLICATION_NAME: !Ref ApplicationName
          OWNER: !Ref Owner

  CognitoPreAuthLambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-preauth-role-${Environment}'
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-preauth-policy-${Environment}'
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: '*'

  CognitoPostAuthLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-postauth-${Environment}'
      Handler: index.handler
      Role: !GetAtt CognitoPostAuthLambdaRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          def handler(event, context):
              return event
      Runtime: python3.12
      Timeout: 3
      MemorySize: 128
      Environment:
        Variables:
          ENVIRONMENT: !Ref Environment
          APPLICATION_NAME: !Ref ApplicationName
          OWNER: !Ref Owner

  CognitoPostAuthLambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-postauth-role-${Environment}'
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-postauth-policy-${Environment}'
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: '*'

  CognitoCustomMessageLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-custommessage-${Environment}'
      Handler: index.handler
      Role: !GetAtt CognitoCustomMessageLambdaRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          def handler(event, context):
              return event
      Runtime: python3.12
      Timeout: 3
      MemorySize: 128
      Environment:
        Variables:
          ENVIRONMENT: !Ref Environment
          APPLICATION_NAME: !Ref ApplicationName
          OWNER: !Ref Owner

  CognitoCustomMessageLambdaRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-custommessage-role-${Environment}'
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-custommessage-policy-${Environment}'
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: '*'

Outputs:
  UserPoolId:
    Description: 'The ID of the Cognito User Pool'
    Value: !Ref CognitoUserPool
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-UserPoolId-${Environment}'
  UserPoolArn:
    Description: 'The ARN of the Cognito User Pool'
    Value: !GetAtt CognitoUserPool.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-UserPoolArn-${Environment}'
  UserPoolDomainUrl:
    Description: 'The domain URL of the Cognito Hosted UI'
    Value: !Sub 'https://${CloudFrontDistributionArn}'
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${ApplicationName}-UserPoolDomain-${Environment}'
  UserPoolClientId:
    Description: 'The App Client ID for the Cognito User Pool'
    Value: !Ref CognitoUserPoolClient

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: EventBridge to MongoDB integration with API key auth, dead-letter queue, and retry configuration.

Resources:
  ApplicationEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: ApplicationEventBus
      Tags:
        - Key: Application
          Value: EventBridgeMongoDBIntegration

  EventBridgeToMongoDBRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: EventBridgeToMongoDBRole
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: InvokeMongoDBApiPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - events:InvokeApiDestination
                  - events:PutEvents
                Resource:
                  - !GetAtt ApplicationEventBus.Arn
                  - !GetAtt MongoDBApiDestination.Arn

  MongoDBAPIConnection:
    Type: AWS::Events::Connection
    Properties:
      Name: MongoDBAPIConnection
      AuthorizationType: API_KEY
      AuthParameters:
        ApiKeyAuthParameters:
          ApiKeyName: test_name
          ApiKeyValue: test_value
      Description: Connection for routing events to MongoDB API endpoint

  MongoDBApiDestination:
    Type: AWS::Events::ApiDestination
    Properties:
      Name: MongoDBApiDestination
      ConnectionArn: !Ref MongoDBAPIConnection
      InvocationEndpoint: https://www.testanu.com
      InvocationRateLimitPerSecond: 5
      HttpMethod: POST
      Description: API destination for MongoDB event processing

  MongoDBEventDeadLetterQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: MongoDBEventDeadLetterQueue
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600
      KmsMasterKeyId: alias/aws/sqs
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: DeadLetterQueueForMongoDBEvents

  EventBridgeRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: MongoDBEventRoutingRule
      EventBusName: !Ref ApplicationEventBus
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - app.event
      Targets:
        - - Arn: !GetAtt MongoDBApiDestination.Arn
            Id: MongoDBApiDestinationTarget
            RetryPolicy:
              MaximumEventAgeInSeconds: 86400
              MaximumRetryAttempts: 180
            DeadLetterConfig:
              TargetArn: !GetAtt MongoDBEventDeadLetterQueue.Arn
      State: ENABLED
      Description: Route custom application events to MongoDB API endpoint with retry and dead-letter handling

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Cross-account private API Gateway integrated with Lambda, secured via VPC endpoint and custom domain.

Parameters:
  VpcEndpointId:
    Type: String
    Description: The VPC endpoint ID that is allowed to access the private API Gateway.
  DomainName:
    Type: String
    Description: The custom domain name for the API Gateway (e.g., api.example.com).
  CertificateArn:
    Type: String
    Description: The ARN of the ACM certificate for the custom domain.
  EnvironmentName:
    Type: String
    Default: dev
    Description: Environment tag for cost tracking and governance.

Resources:
  # Lambda Execution Role
  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole
      Path: /
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-LambdaExecutionRole-${EnvironmentName}'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName

  # Lambda Function
  HelloLambdaFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-HelloFunction-${EnvironmentName}'
      Runtime: python3.14
      Handler: index.handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          def handler(event, context):
              return {
                  'statusCode': 200,
                  'headers': {
                      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                      'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*'
                  },
                  'body': json.dumps({'message': 'Hello from private API Gateway via Lambda!'})
              }
      Environment:
        Variables:
          ENVIRONMENT: !Ref EnvironmentName
      MemorySize: 128
      Timeout: 30
      TracingConfig:
        Mode: Active
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-HelloLambda-${EnvironmentName}'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName

  # Lambda Permission for API Gateway
  ApiGatewayInvokeLambdaPermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt HelloLambdaFunction.Arn
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      Principal: apigateway.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !Sub 'arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:${ApiGatewayRestApi}/*/*/*'

  # Private API Gateway REST API
  ApiGatewayRestApi:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-PrivateApi-${EnvironmentName}'
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Types:
          - PRIVATE

  # API Gateway Resource
  ApiGatewayResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayRestApi
      ParentId: !GetAtt ApiGatewayRestApi.RootResourceId
      PathPart: hello

  # API Gateway Method
  ApiGatewayMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayRestApi
      ResourceId: !Ref ApiGatewayResource
      HttpMethod: GET
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      Integration:
        Type: AWS_PROXY
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub 'arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${HelloLambdaFunction.Arn}/invocations'
      MethodResponses:
        - StatusCode: 200
          ResponseParameters:
            method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin: true
      RequestParameters:
          integration.request.header X-Forwarded-For: 'method.request.header.X-Forwarded-For'

  # API Gateway Deployment
  ApiGatewayDeployment:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Deployment
    DependsOn: ApiGatewayMethod
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayRestApi
      StageName: prod

  # Custom Domain Name
  ApiGatewayDomainName:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::DomainName
    Properties:
      DomainName: !Ref DomainName
      RegionalCertificateArn: !Ref CertificateArn
      SecurityPolicy: TLS_1_2
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Types:
          - REGIONAL
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-ApiDomain-${EnvironmentName}'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName

  # Base Path Mapping
  ApiGatewayBasePathMapping:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::BasePathMapping
    Properties:
      DomainName: !Ref DomainName
      RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayRestApi
      Stage: prod

  # VPC Endpoint with inline policy
  VPCEndpoint:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPCEndpoint
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref VpcEndpointId
      ServiceName: !Sub 'com.amazonaws.${AWS::Region}.execute-api'
      Policy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal: '*'
            Action:
              - execute-api:Invoke
            Resource:
              - !Sub 'arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:${ApiGatewayRestApi}/*/*/*'
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                aws:SourceVpce: !Ref VpcEndpointId
          - Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action:
              - execute-api:Invoke
            Resource:
              - !Sub 'arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:${ApiGatewayRestApi}/*/*/*'
            Condition:
              StringNotEquals:
                aws:SourceVpce: !Ref VpcEndpointId

  # Stage with tags via separate Tags resource
  ApiGatewayStage:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Stage
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayRestApi
      DeploymentId: !Ref ApiGatewayDeployment
      StageName: prod
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-ApiStage-${EnvironmentName}'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref EnvironmentName

Outputs:
  LambdaFunctionArn:
    Description: ARN of the Lambda function
    Value: !GetAtt HelloLambdaFunction.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-LambdaArn-${EnvironmentName}'

  ApiGatewayInvokeUrl:
    Description: Invoke URL for the private API Gateway stage
    Value: !Sub 'https://${ApiGatewayRestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/prod/hello/'

  CustomDomainName:
    Description: Custom domain name for the API
    Value: !Ref DomainName
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-CustomDomain-${EnvironmentName}'

  VpcEndpointId:
    Description: VPC Endpoint ID used for access restriction
    Value: !Ref VpcEndpointId
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-VpcEndpointId-${EnvironmentName}'

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: "CloudFormation template to create EventBridge integration with DataDog for centralized log forwarding, including an event bus, API destination, secure connection, IAM role, and dead-letter SQS queue."

Resources:
  MyDataDogAppEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: MyDataDogAppEventBus

  DataDogConnection:
    Type: AWS::Events::Connection
    Properties:
      Name: DataDogConnection
      AuthorizationType: API_KEY
      AuthParameters:
        ApiKeyAuthParameters:
          ApiKeyName: apiKey
          ApiKeyValue: testanu
      Description: "Connection for secure DataDog log ingestion"

  DataDogLogApiDestination:
    Type: AWS::Events::ApiDestination
    Properties:
      Name: DataDogLogApiDestination
      ConnectionArn: !GetAtt DataDogConnection.Arn
      InvocationEndpoint: https://http-intake.logs.datadoghq.com
      InvocationRateLimitPerSecond: 5
      HttpMethod: POST

  EventBridgeToDataDogRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: EventBridgeInvokeDataDogDestination
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - events:InvokeApiDestination
                  - events:ListApiDestinations
                Resource: !GetAtt DataDogLogApiDestination.Arn

  EventBridgeDeadLetterQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-eventbridge-dead-letter-queue"
      RedrivePolicy:
        deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueueDLQ.Arn
        maxReceiveCount: 3
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref DeadLetterQueueKMSKey

  DeadLetterQueueDLQ:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-dead-letter-dlq"
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref DeadLetterQueueKMSKey

  DeadLetterQueueKMSKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: "Customer-managed KMS key for dead-letter queue encryption"
      EnableKeyRotation: true
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Id: key-default-1
        Statement:
          - Sid: Allow administration of the key
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action:
              - kms:*
            Resource: "*"

  EventBridgeRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: DataDogLogForwardingRule
      EventBusName: !Ref MyDataDogAppEventBus
      Targets:
        - Id: DataDogLogTarget
          Arn: !GetAtt DataDogLogApiDestination.Arn
          RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeToDataDogRole.Arn
          DeadLetterConfig:
            Arn: !GetAtt EventBridgeDeadLetterQueue.Arn
      State: ENABLED
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - MyDataDogApp

Outputs:
  EventBusArn:
    Description: "ARN of the EventBridge event bus"
    Value: !GetAtt MyDataDogAppEventBus.Arn
  ApiDestinationArn:
    Description: "ARN of the DataDog API destination"
    Value: !GetAtt DataDogLogApiDestination.Arn
  ConnectionArn:
    Description: "ARN of the EventBridge connection"
    Value: !GetAtt DataDogConnection.Arn
  DeadLetterQueueUrl:
    Description: "URL of the dead-letter SQS queue"
    Value: !GetAtt EventBridgeDeadLetterQueue.QueueUrl

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Creates an S3 bucket with customer-managed KMS encryption, public access block, and an SQS queue configured for object creation event notifications.'

Conditions:
  NotUsEast1: !Not [!Equals [!Ref 'AWS::Region', 'us-east-1']]

Resources:
  KMSKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: !Sub 'KMS key for ${AWS::StackName} S3 and SQS encryption'
      EnableKeyRotation: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: Encryption
        - Key: Stack
          Value: !Ref 'AWS::StackName'

  KMSKeyAlias:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Alias
    Properties:
      AliasName: !Sub 'alias/${AWS::StackName}'
      TargetKeyId: !Ref KMSKey

  S3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub '${AWS::Region}-${AWS::AccountId}-object-upload-bucket'
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: aws:kms
              KMSMasterKeyID: !Ref KMSKey
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: ObjectStorage
        - Key: Stack
          Value: !Ref 'AWS::StackName'

  SQSQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-object-upload-queue'
      VisibilityTimeout: 30
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref KMSKey
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: EventProcessing
        - Key: Stack
          Value: !Ref 'AWS::StackName'

  BucketQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref SQSQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowS3SendMessage
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: s3.amazonaws.com
            Action: sqs:SendMessage
            Resource: !GetAtt SQSQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              ArnEquals:
                aws:SourceArn: !GetAtt S3Bucket.Arn

  BucketNotification:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketNotification
    Condition: NotUsEast1
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref S3Bucket
      QueueConfigurations:
        - Event:
            - s3:ObjectCreated:Put
            - s3:ObjectCreated:Post
            - s3:ObjectCreated:Copy
            - s3:ObjectCreated:CompleteMultipartUpload
          Queue: !GetAtt SQSQueue.Arn
          Filter: {}

  KMSKeyPolicyS3:
    Type: AWS::KMS::KeyPolicy
    Condition: NotUsEast1
    Properties:
      KeyId: !Ref KMSKey
      Policy: !Sub
        - |
          {
            "Version": "2012-10-17",
            "Id": "key-policy-s3",
            "Statement": [
              {
                "Sid": "Enable IAM User Permissions",
                "Effect": "Allow",
                "Principal": {
                  "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
                },
                "Action": "kms:*",
                "Resource": "*"
              },
              {
                "Sid": "Allow S3 to use key",
                "Effect": "Allow",
                "Principal": {
                  "Service": "s3.amazonaws.com"
                },
                "Action": [
                  "kms:GenerateDataKey",
                  "kms:Decrypt"
                ],
                "Resource": "*"
              }
            ]
          }
        - {}

  KMSKeyPolicySQS:
    Type: AWS::KMS::KeyPolicy
    Condition: NotUsEast1
    Properties:
      KeyId: !Ref KMSKey
      Policy: !Sub
        - |
          {
            "Version": "2012-10-17",
            "Id": "key-policy-sqs",
            "Statement": [
              {
                "Sid": "Enable IAM User Permissions",
                "Effect": "Allow",
                "Principal": {
                  "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
                },
                "Action": "kms:*",
                "Resource": "*"
              },
              {
                "Sid": "Allow SQS to use key",
                "Effect": "Allow",
                "Principal": {
                  "Service": "sqs.amazonaws.com"
                },
                "Action": [
                  "kms:GenerateDataKey",
                  "kms:Encrypt",
                  "kms:Decrypt"
                ],
                "Resource": "*"
              }
            ]
          }
        - {}

Outputs:
  S3BucketName:
    Description: 'Name of the S3 bucket'
    Value: !Ref S3Bucket
  SQSQueueURL:
    Description: 'URL of the SQS queue'
    Value: !Ref SQSQueue
  SQSQueueArn:
    Description: 'ARN of the SQS queue'
    Value: !GetAtt SQSQueue.Arn
  KMSKeyArn:
    Description: 'ARN of the KMS key'
    Value: !GetAtt KMSKey.Arn

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Creates an EventBridge rule with an SNS topic for demo.cli events.'

Resources:
  EventBridgeSnsTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-eventbridge-sns-topic'
      KmsMasterKeyId: 'aws/sns'
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Production
        - Key: Application
          Value: EventBridgeSnsIntegration
        - Key: Owner
          Value: CloudFormation
      Subscription: []

  EventBridgeRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-eventbridge-rule'
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - 'demo.cli'
      Targets:
        - Arn: !Ref EventBridgeSnsTopic
          Id: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-sns-target'
      State: ENABLED
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Production
        - Key: Application
          Value: EventBridgeSnsIntegration
        - Key: Owner
          Value: CloudFormation

  EventBridgeSnsTopicPolicy:
    Type: AWS::SNS::TopicPolicy
    Properties:
      Topics:
        - !Ref EventBridgeSnsTopic
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowEventBridgePublish
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sns:Publish
            Resource: !Ref EventBridgeSnsTopic
            Condition:
              ArnEquals:

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'B2BI Pipeline for EDI X12_214 to JSON transformation with S3 input/output'

Parameters:
  BucketSuffix:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Unique suffix for S3 bucket names to ensure global uniqueness'
    Default: 'b2bi-data'
  ContactName:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Business contact name for B2BI profile'
    Default: 'Business Contact'
  ContactPhone:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Business contact phone for B2BI profile'
    Default: '+1-555-123-4567'

Resources:
  InputS3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-${BucketSuffix}-${AWS::Region}-input'
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackName
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: InputRawEDI

  InputS3BucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref InputS3Bucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: EnforceHTTPS
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 's3:*'
            Resource:
              - !GetAtt InputS3Bucket.Arn
              - !Sub '${InputS3Bucket.Arn}/*'
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: 'false'
          - Sid: AllowEncryptedTransportOnly
            Effect: Allow
            Principal: '*'
            Action:
              - 's3:GetBucket*'
              - 's3:List*'
              - 's3:PutBucketPolicy'
            Resource: !GetAtt InputS3Bucket.Arn
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: 'true'

  OutputS3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-${BucketSuffix}-${AWS::Region}-output'
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackName
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: OutputTransformedJSON

  OutputS3BucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref OutputS3Bucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: EnforceHTTPS
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 's3:*'
            Resource:
              - !GetAtt OutputS3Bucket.Arn
              - !Sub '${OutputS3Bucket.Arn}/*'
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: 'false'
          - Sid: AllowEncryptedTransportOnly
            Effect: Allow
            Principal: '*'
            Action:
              - 's3:GetBucket*'
              - 's3:List*'
              - 's3:PutBucketPolicy'
            Resource: !GetAtt OutputS3Bucket.Arn
            Condition:
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: 'true'

  B2BIIAMRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-B2BI-ExecutionRole'
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: b2bi.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonS3ReadOnlyAccess
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: B2BIS3Access
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - 's3:GetObject'
                  - 's3:PutObject'
                  - 's3:DeleteObject'
                  - 's3:ListBucket'
                Resource:
                  - !GetAtt InputS3Bucket.Arn
                  - !Sub '${InputS3Bucket.Arn}/*'
                  - !GetAtt OutputS3Bucket.Arn
                  - !Sub '${OutputS3Bucket.Arn}/*'
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - 'b2bi:*'
                Resource: '*'
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackName
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: B2BIExecutionRole

  B2BIProfile:
    Type: AWS::B2BI::Profile
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-EDI-Profile'
      BusinessName: !Ref ContactName
      Phone: !Ref ContactPhone
      Logging: ENABLED
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackName
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: B2BIProfile

  EDIToJSONTransformer:
    Type: AWS::B2BI::Transformer
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-EDIToJSON'
      Status: active
      Mapping:
        - TemplateLanguage: JSONATA
        - Type: segment_to_json
          Source: ISA
          Target: interchange_control
        - Type: segment_to_json
          Source: GS
          Target: functional_group
        - Type: segment_to_json
          Source: ST
          Target: transaction_set_header
        - Type: loop_to_json
          Source: LX
          Target: transaction_detail_loop
        - Type: element_mapping
          Source: B12
          Target: quantity_and_weight
        - Type: segment_to_json
          Source: SE
          Target: transaction_set_trailer
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackName
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: EDIToJSONTransformer

Outputs:
  InputS3BucketName:
    Description: 'Name of the input S3 bucket for raw EDI files'
    Value: !Ref InputS3Bucket

  OutputS3BucketName:
    Description: 'Name of the output S3 bucket for transformed JSON files'
    Value: !Ref OutputS3Bucket

  B2BIProfileARN:
    Description: 'ARN of the B2BI profile'
    Value: !GetAtt B2BIProfile.ProfileArn

  EDIToJSONTransformerARN:
    Description: 'ARN of the EDI to JSON transformer'
    Value: !GetAtt EDIToJSONTransformer.TransformerArn

  B2BIIAMRoleARN:
    Description: 'ARN of the B2BI IAM execution role'
    Value: !GetAtt B2BIIAMRole.Arn

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Automated monitoring and alerting system for AWS Secrets Manager activities with email notifications.'

Metadata:
  AWS::CloudFormation::Interface:
    ParameterGroups:
      - Label:
          default: 'Notification Configuration'
        Parameters:
          - AlertEmailAddress
    ParameterLabels:
      AlertEmailAddress:
        default: 'Email Address for SNS Notifications'

Parameters:
  AlertEmailAddress:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Email address to receive Secrets Manager alert notifications.'
    Default: 'alert@example.com'
    AllowedPattern: '^.*$'

Resources:
  SecretsManagerKMSKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: Customer-managed KMS key for SNS topic encryption
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Id: key-default-1
        Statement:
          - Sid: Enable IAM User Permissions
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action: 'kms:*'
            Resource: '*'

  SecretsManagerAlertsTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      DisplayName: SecretsManagerAlertsTopic
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref SecretsManagerKMSKey
      Subscription:
        - Endpoint: !Ref AlertEmailAddress
          Protocol: email
    DeletionPolicy: Retain
    UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain

  SecretsManagerEventsRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole

  SecretsManagerEventsPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Policy
    Properties:
      PolicyName: SecretsManagerEventsPublishPolicy
      Roles:
        - !Ref SecretsManagerEventsRole
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Action: sns:Publish
            Resource: !Ref SecretsManagerAlertsTopic
          - Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - kms:GenerateDataKey
              - kms:Decrypt
            Resource: !Ref SecretsManagerKMSKey

  SecretsManagerApiEventsRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Description: Captures AWS Secrets Manager API activity and routes to SNS for alerts.
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - aws.secretsmanager
        detail-type:
          - AWS API Call via CloudTrail
      RoleArn: !GetAtt SecretsManagerEventsRole.Arn
      State: ENABLED
      Targets:
        - Arn: !Ref SecretsManagerAlertsTopic
          Id: SecretsManagerAlertsTarget
          InputTransformer:
            InputPathsMap:
              api: $.detail.eventName
              secret: $.detail.requestParameters.secretId
              user: $.detail.userIdentity.arn
              time: $.detail.eventTime
              region: $.region
            InputTemplate: |
              {
                "api": <api>,
                "secret": <secret>,
                "user": <user>,
                "time": <time>,
                "region": <region>
              }

Outputs:
  SnsTopicArn:
    Description: 'ARN of the SNS topic for Secrets Manager alerts.'
    Value: !Ref SecretsManagerAlertsTopic
    Export:
      Name: SecretsManagerAlertsTopicArn

  SnsTopicKmsKeyArn:
    Description: 'ARN of the customer-managed KMS key used for SNS encryption.'
    Value: !Ref SecretsManagerKMSKey
    Export:
      Name: SecretsManagerKMSKeyArn

  SubscriptionConfirmationStatus:
    Description: 'Status of the email subscription to the SNS topic.'
    Value: !Sub 'Pending confirmation. Please check email ${AlertEmailAddress} to confirm subscription.'

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  SNS-to-SQS fanout system with filtered message routing for metric types.
  Includes a central SNS topic, three SQS queues (all metrics, temperature, humidity),
  dead-letter queues, customer-managed KMS encryption, and filtered subscriptions.

Resources:
  # ── KMS Keys ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  SnsKmsKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: Customer-managed KMS key for SNS topic encryption
      EnableKeyRotation: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Application
          Value: MetricsFanout
        - Key: StackId
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackId

  SqsKmsKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: Customer-managed KMS key for SQS queue encryption
      EnableKeyRotation: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Application
          Value: MetricsFanout
        - Key: StackId
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackId

  # ── Central SNS Topic ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  MetricsTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      DisplayName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-MetricsTopic'
      TopicName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-MetricsTopic'
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref SnsKmsKey
      Tags:
        - Key: Application
          Value: MetricsFanout
        - Key: StackId
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackId

  # ── Dead-Letter Queues ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  AllMetricsQueueDLQ:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-AllMetricsQueue-DLQ'
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600  # 14 days
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref SqsKmsKey
      Tags:
        - Key: QueueType
          Value: DLQ
        - Key: StackId
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackId

  TemperatureSqsQueueDLQ:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-TemperatureSqsQueue-DLQ'
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref SqsKmsKey
      Tags:
        - Key: QueueType
          Value: DLQ
        - Key: StackId
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackId

  HumiditySqsQueueDLQ:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-HumiditySqsQueue-DLQ'
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref SqsKmsKey
      Tags:
        - Key: QueueType
          Value: DLQ
        - Key: StackId
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackId

  # ── Main Processing Queues ───────────────────────────────────────────
  AllMetricsQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-AllMetricsQueue'
      RedrivePolicy:
        deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt AllMetricsQueueDLQ.Arn
        maxReceiveCount: 5
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref SqsKmsKey
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600  # 4 days
      VisibilityTimeout: 30
      Tags:
        - Key: QueueType
          Value: AllMetrics
        - Key: StackId
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackId

  TemperatureSqsQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-TemperatureSqsQueue'
      RedrivePolicy:
        deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt TemperatureSqsQueueDLQ.Arn
        maxReceiveCount: 5
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref SqsKmsKey
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600
      VisibilityTimeout: 30
      Tags:
        - Key: MetricType
          Value: Temperature
        - Key: StackId
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackId

  HumiditySqsQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-HumiditySqsQueue'
      RedrivePolicy:
        deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt HumiditySqsQueueDLQ.Arn
        maxReceiveCount: 5
      KmsMasterKeyId: !Ref SqsKmsKey
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600
      VisibilityTimeout: 30
      Tags:
        - Key: MetricType
          Value: Humidity
        - Key: StackId
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackId

  # ── SNS Subscriptions ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  AllMetricsSubscription:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription
    Properties:
      Endpoint: !GetAtt AllMetricsQueue.Arn
      Protocol: sqs
      TopicArn: !Ref MetricsTopic
      RawMessageDelivery: true
      FilterPolicy:
        # Empty filter policy means “match all messages”.
        # Explicitly defined as an empty map to satisfy schema requirements
        # while preserving the “all messages” behavior.
        {}

  TemperatureSubscription:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription
    Properties:
      Endpoint: !GetAtt TemperatureSqsQueue.Arn
      Protocol: sqs
      TopicArn: !Ref MetricsTopic
      RawMessageDelivery: true
      FilterPolicy:
        MetricType:
          - Temperature

  HumiditySubscription:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription
    Properties:
      Endpoint: !GetAtt HumiditySqsQueue.Arn
      Protocol: sqs
      TopicArn: !Ref MetricsTopic
      RawMessageDelivery: true
      FilterPolicy:
        MetricType:
          - Humidity

  # ── IAM Permissions (SNS → SQS) ──────────────────────────────────────
  AllMetricsQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref AllMetricsQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowSNSToSendAllMetrics
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: '*'
            Action: sqs:SendMessage
            Resource: !GetAtt AllMetricsQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              ArnEquals:
                aws:SourceArn: !Ref MetricsTopic

  TemperatureQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref TemperatureSqsQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowSNSToSendTemperature
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: '*'
            Action: sqs:SendMessage
            Resource: !GetAtt TemperatureSqsQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              ArnEquals:
                aws:SourceArn: !Ref MetricsTopic

  HumidityQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref HumiditySqsQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowSNSToSendHumidity
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: '*'
            Action: sqs:SendMessage
            Resource: !GetAtt HumiditySqsQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              ArnEquals:
                aws:SourceArn: !Ref MetricsTopic

Outputs:
  MetricsTopicArn:
    Description: ARN of the central SNS topic
    Value: !Ref MetricsTopic

  AllMetricsQueueUrl:
    Description: URL of the all-metrics SQS queue
    Value: !GetAtt AllMetricsQueue.QueueUrl

  TemperatureSqsQueueUrl:
    Description: URL of the temperature-filtered SQS queue
    Value: !GetAtt TemperatureSqsQueue.QueueUrl

  HumiditySqsQueueUrl:
    Description: URL of the humidity-filtered SQS queue
    Value: !GetAtt HumiditySqsQueue.QueueUrl

  AllMetricsSubscriptionId:
    Description: Subscription ID for all-metrics SNS→SQS subscription
    Value: !Ref AllMetricsSubscription

  TemperatureSubscriptionId:
    Description: Subscription ID for temperature-filtered SNS→SQS subscription
    Value: !Ref TemperatureSubscription

  HumiditySubscriptionId:
    Description: Subscription ID for humidity-filtered SNS→SQS subscription
    Value: !Ref HumiditySubscription

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: Event-driven integration between DynamoDB Streams and EventBridge

Resources:
  DynamoDBTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-OrdersTable'
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: orderId
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: orderId
          KeyType: HASH
      BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
      StreamSpecification:
        StreamViewType: NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES
      SSESpecification:
        SSEEnabled: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: EventDrivenIntegration
        - Key: StackName
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackName

  EventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:

  PipeRole:

                  - events:PutEvents
                Resource: !GetAtt EventBus.Arn

Outputs:

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Event-driven data enrichment workflow using EventBridge Rule, Lambda, SQS,
  Events Connection + ApiDestination, CloudWatch Log Group, and DLQ.
  Region-aware to work in us-east-1 where AWS::Events::Pipe is unavailable.

Parameters:
  StackName:
    Type: String
    Description: Name of the stack used for naming resources.
  LogGroupRetentionDays:
    Type: Number
    Default: 30
    Description: Retention period in days for the CloudWatch Log Group.
  MessageTtl:
    Type: Number
    Default: 345600
    Description: Message retention period in seconds for the SQS queue (max 1209600).
  ApiEndpointOverride:
    Type: String
    Default: ''
    Description: Optional override for the external API endpoint (e.g., for testing).
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Default: dev
    Description: Environment tag (e.g., dev, staging, prod).
  Owner:
    Type: String
    Default: platform-team
    Description: Owner tag for cost allocation.

Resources:
  # SQS Queue with server-side encryption using AWS-managed KMS key
  EventSourceQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${StackName}-enrichment-queue'
      KmsMasterKeyId: alias/aws/sqs
      VisibilityTimeout: 30
      MessageRetentionPeriod: !Ref MessageTtl
      RedrivePolicy:
        deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueue.Arn
        maxReceiveCount: 3

  # Dead-letter queue for failed messages
  DeadLetterQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${StackName}-enrichment-dlq'
      KmsMasterKeyId: alias/aws/sqs
      MessageRetentionPeriod: !Ref MessageTtl

  # CloudWatch Log Group for enriched message logs
  EnrichedLogGroup:
    Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup
    Properties:
      LogGroupName: !Sub '/${StackName}/enriched-messages'
      RetentionInDays: !Ref LogGroupRetentionDays

  # Events Connection for secure external API invocation
  ApiConnection:
    Type: AWS::Events::Connection
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${StackName}-api-connection'
      AuthorizationType: API_KEY
      AuthParameters:
        ApiKeyAuthParameters:
          ApiKeyName: x-api-key
          ApiKeyValue: !If [HasOverride, 'override-key-placeholder', 'zippopotam-us-key']
      Description: Connection for zippopotam.us API
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Owner
          Value: !Ref Owner

  # API Destination using updated schema
  ApiDestination:
    Type: AWS::Events::ApiDestination
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${StackName}-api-destination'
      ConnectionArn: !GetAtt ApiConnection.Arn
      InvocationEndpoint: !If
        - HasOverride
        - !Ref ApiEndpointOverride
        - 'https://api.zippopotam.us'
      InvocationRateLimitPerSecond: 10
      HttpMethod: POST
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Owner
          Value: !Ref Owner

  # Lambda function to perform enrichment
  EnrichmentFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub '${StackName}-enrichment-lambda'
      Handler: index.handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Runtime: python3.12
      Timeout: 30
      MemorySize: 256
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import os
          import urllib.request

          def handler(event, context):
              results = []
              for record in event.get('Records', []):
                  payload = json.loads(record['body'])
                  zip_code = str(payload.get('zipCode', ''))
                  original = payload.get('originalMessage', {})

                  if not zip_code:
                      results.append({'status': 'SKIPPED', 'reason': 'missing zipCode', 'original': original})
                      continue

                  api_root = os.environ.get('API_ROOT', 'https://api.zippopotam.us')
                  url = f"{api_root}/us/{zip_code}"
                  try:
                      req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'CloudFormation-Template'})
                      with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as resp:
                          data = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
                      enriched = {**original, 'zipCode': zip_code, 'locationData': data}
                      results.append({'status': 'ENRICHED', 'enriched': enriched})
                  except Exception as e:
                      results.append({'status': 'ERROR', 'error': str(e), 'original': original})

              # Send enriched results to CloudWatch Logs via CloudWatch Logs subscription
              print(json.dumps({'enrichmentResults': results}))
              return {'statusCode': 200, 'results': results}

  # Lambda Execution Role
  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: LambdaExecutionPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: !Sub '${EnrichedLogGroup.Arn}:*'
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - events:InvokeApiDestination
                  - events:PutEvents
                Resource: '*'
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - sqs:ReceiveMessage
                  - sqs:DeleteMessage
                  - sqs:GetQueueAttributes
                  - sqs:ListDeadLetterSourceQueues
                Resource: !GetAtt EventSourceQueue.Arn

  # EventBridge Rule to trigger Lambda with SQS as source
  EnrichmentRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${StackName}-enrichment-rule'
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - aws.sqs
        detail-type:
          - AWS API Call via CloudTrail
        detail:
          eventSource:
            - sqs.amazonaws.com
          eventName:
            - SendMessage
      Targets:
        - Id: EnrichmentTarget
          Arn: !GetAtt EnrichmentFunction.Arn
          InputTransformer:
            InputPathsMap:
              body: $.detail.requestParameters.messageBody
            InputTemplate: |
              {
                "Records": [
                  {
                    "body": <body>,
                    "messageId": "1",
                    "receiptHandle": "placeholder"
                  }
                ]
              }

  # Lambda Permission for EventBridge to invoke
  LambdaInvokePermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref EnrichmentFunction
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      Principal: events.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !GetAtt EnrichmentRule.Arn

Conditions:
  HasOverride: !Not [!Equals [!Ref ApiEndpointOverride, '']]

Outputs:
  QueueUrl:
    Description: URL of the SQS queue
    Value: !Ref EventSourceQueue
  PipeName:
    Description: Name of the enrichment workflow (implemented via EventBridge Rule + Lambda)
    Value: !Sub '${StackName}-enrichment-rule'
  LogGroupName:
    Description: CloudWatch Log Group for enriched messages
    Value: !Ref EnrichedLogGroup
  DeadLetterQueueUrl:
    Description: URL of the dead-letter queue
    Value: !Ref DeadLetterQueue

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'CloudFormation template for EventBridge-to-Kinesis integration for real-time event streaming'

Parameters:
  EventSourceName:
    Type: String
    Default: 'my-application'
    Description: 'The name of the event source to capture events from'
  StreamName:
    Type: String
    Default: 'ApplicationEventStream'
    Description: 'Name of the Kinesis Data Stream'
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Default: 'dev'
    Description: 'Environment label for tagging resources'

Resources:
  ApplicationEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: ApplicationEventBus
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Name
          Value: ApplicationEventBus

  ApplicationEventStream:
    Type: AWS::Kinesis::Stream
    Properties:
      Name: !Ref StreamName
      ShardCount: 1
      StreamEncryption:
        EncryptionType: KMS
        KeyId: !GetAtt KMSKey.Arn
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Name
          Value: ApplicationEventStream

  KMSKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      EnableKeyRotation: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Name
          Value: ApplicationEventStreamKey

  EventBridgeToKinesisRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: EventBridgeToKinesisPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - kinesis:PutRecord
                  - kinesis:PutRecords
                Resource: !GetAtt ApplicationEventStream.Arn
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Name
          Value: EventBridgeToKinesisRole

  EventBridgeRule:
    Type: AWS::Events::Rule
    Properties:
      Name: ApplicationEventRoutingRule
      EventBusName: !Ref ApplicationEventBus
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - !Ref EventSourceName
      Targets:
        - Id: KinesisTarget
          Arn: !GetAtt ApplicationEventStream.Arn
          RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeToKinesisRole.Arn
          InputTransformer:
            InputPathsMap:
              detail: "$.detail"
            InputTemplate: !Sub |
              {
                "data": $.detail,
                "source": "${EventSourceName}"
              }
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Name
          Value: EventBridgeRule

Outputs:
  EventBusName:
    Description: 'Name of the EventBridge EventBus'
    Value: !Ref ApplicationEventBus
  StreamName:
    Description: 'Name of the Kinesis Data Stream'
    Value: !Ref ApplicationEventStream
  StreamArn:
    Description: 'ARN of the Kinesis Data Stream'
    Value: !GetAtt ApplicationEventStream.Arn
  IAMRoleArn:
    Description: 'ARN of the IAM Role for EventBridge to Kinesis'
    Value: !GetAtt EventBridgeToKinesisRole.Arn

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: Event-driven AWS Batch job processing pipeline with SQS, EventBridge Pipes, VPC, and security configurations.

Parameters:
  VpcCidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.0.0/16
    Description: CIDR block for the VPC.
  PrivateSubnet1Cidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.1.0/24
    Description: CIDR block for private subnet 1.
  PrivateSubnet2Cidr:
    Type: String
    Default: 10.0.2.0/24
    Description: CIDR block for private subnet 2.

Resources:
  EventDrivenBatchVPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: !Ref VpcCidr
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: EventDrivenBatchVPC

  PrivateSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref EventDrivenBatchVPC
      CidrBlock: !Ref PrivateSubnet1Cidr
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PrivateSubnet1

  PrivateSubnet2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref EventDrivenBatchVPC
      CidrBlock: !Ref PrivateSubnet2Cidr
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [1, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PrivateSubnet2

  BatchSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for AWS Batch compute resources
      VpcId: !Ref EventDrivenBatchVPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

        - IpProtocol: -1

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Scheduled automation workflow using EventBridge Scheduler and SSM to execute cross-platform
  scripts on tagged EC2 instances. Creates Linux and Windows EC2 instances (SSM enabled),
  SSM Document, SSM State Manager Association, EventBridge Schedule, IAM roles, and S3 log bucket.

Parameters:
  KeyPairName:
    Description: Name of an existing EC2 key pair for SSH/RDP access (optional).
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Default: ''
  InstanceType:
    Description: EC2 instance type for both Linux and Windows.
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
  LinuxAmiId:
    Description: Pre-approved Linux AMI ID (e.g., Amazon Linux 2 in the region).
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value<AWS::EC2::Image::Id>
    Default: /aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/al2023-ami-kernel-default-x86_64
  WindowsAmiId:
    Description: Pre-approved Windows Server AMI ID.
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value<AWS::EC2::Image::Id>
    Default: /aws/service/ami-windows-latest/Windows_Server-2022-English-Full-Base

Conditions:
  HasKeyPair: !Not [!Equals [!Ref KeyPairName, '']]

Resources:
  # S3 bucket for execution logs
  LogBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled

  # IAM Role for EC2 instances
  EC2InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: SSMAndS3Access
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ssm:SendCommand
                  - ssm:DescribeInstanceInformation
                  - ssm:ListCommands
                  - ssm:GetCommandInvocation
                Resource: '*'
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:GetObject
                Resource: !Sub 'arn:aws:s3:::${LogBucket}/*'

  # IAM Role for EventBridge Scheduler
  EventBridgeSchedulerRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: EventBridgeToSSMAutomation
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ssm:SendCommand
                  - ssm:GetAutomationExecution
                  - ssm:DescribeAutomationExecutions
                  - ssm:StartAutomationExecution
                Resource: '*'

  # Linux EC2 instance
  LinuxInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref LinuxAmiId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      KeyName: !If [HasKeyPair, !Ref KeyPairName, !Ref 'AWS::NoValue']
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfileLinux
      Tags:
        - Key: OS
          Value: Linux
        - Key: HelloWorld
          Value: true
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: SSMInstance

  # Windows EC2 instance
  WindowsInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref WindowsAmiId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      KeyName: !If [HasKeyPair, !Ref KeyPairName, !Ref 'AWS::NoValue']
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref EC2InstanceProfileWindows
      Tags:
        - Key: OS
          Value: Windows
        - Key: HelloWorld
          Value: true
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: SSMInstance

  # Instance profiles
  EC2InstanceProfileLinux:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2InstanceRole

  EC2InstanceProfileWindows:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref EC2InstanceRole

  # SSM Document with OS-specific commands
  HelloWorldDocument:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Document
    Properties:
      Content:
        schemaVersion: '2.2'
        description: 'Echo Hello World and log to S3'
        parameters:
          LogBucket:
            type: String
            description: S3 bucket for logs
        mainSteps:
          - action: aws:runShellScript
            name: LinuxHelloWorld
            inputs:
              runCommand:
                - !Sub |
                    echo "Hello World! Linux - $(date)" >> /tmp/helloworld.log
                    echo "Hello World! Linux - $(date)" >> ${LogBucket}/helloworld-linux-$(date +%s).log
              workingDirectory: '~'
            condition: StringEquals
            pattern: 'Linux'
          - action: aws:runPowerShellScript
            name: WindowsHelloWorld
            inputs:
              runPowerShellScript:
                - !Sub |
                    $log = "Hello World! Windows - $(Get-Date)"
                    Write-Output $log
                    $log | Out-File -FilePath "${LogBucket}\helloworld-windows-$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMddHHmmss').log" -Encoding utf8
            condition: StringEquals
            pattern: 'Windows'
      assumeRole: !GetAtt EventBridgeSchedulerRole.Arn
      documentType: Command
      documentFormat: YAML

  # SSM State Manager Association targeting tagged instances
  HelloWorldAssociation:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Association
    Properties:
      AssociationName: !Sub 'HelloWorld-Association-${AWS::StackName}'
      Name: !Ref HelloWorldDocument
      Targets:
        - Key: tag:HelloWorld
          Values:
            - 'true'
      ScheduleExpression: 'rate(1 hour)'
      OutputS3BucketName: !Ref LogBucket
      OutputS3KeyPrefix: ssm-associations
      ComplianceSeverity: UNSPECIFIED
      SyncCompliance: AUTO
      DocumentVersion: '$DEFAULT'
      ApplyOnlyAtCronInterval: false

  # EventBridge Scheduler rule triggering SSM Automation
  HelloWorldSchedule:
    Type: AWS::Scheduler::Schedule
    Properties:
      ScheduleExpression: 'rate(15 minutes)'
      FlexibleTimeWindow:
        Mode: OFF
      Target:
        Arn: !GetAtt EventBridgeSchedulerRole.Arn
        RoleArn: !GetAtt EventBridgeSchedulerRole.Arn
        Input: '{"action":"ssm:SendCommand","documentName":"HelloWorld-Document","parameters":{"commands":["echo \"Hello World! Triggered by EventBridge - $(date)\""],"workingDirectory":"~"},"instanceIds":["TARGETED_BY_TAG"],"outputS3BucketName":"LogBucket","outputS3KeyPrefix":"scheduler-trigger"}'
      Name: !Sub 'HelloWorld-Schedule-${AWS::StackName}'

Outputs:
  LinuxInstanceId:
    Description: Instance ID of the Linux EC2 instance
    Value: !Ref LinuxInstance

  WindowsInstanceId:
    Description: Instance ID of the Windows EC2 instance
    Value: !Ref WindowsInstance

  LogBucketName:
    Description: Name of the S3 bucket storing execution logs
    Value: !Ref LogBucket

  EventBridgeScheduleArn:
    Description: ARN of the EventBridge Scheduler schedule
    Value: !GetAtt HelloWorldSchedule.Arn

  SSMDocumentName:
    Description: Name of the SSM Document
    Value: !Ref HelloWorldDocument

final_template:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'CloudFormation template for EventBridge integration with Shopify product creation API'

Parameters:
  ShopifyAdminApiKey:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Shopify Admin API key for authentication'
    NoEcho: true
    MinLength: 1
    ConstraintDescription: 'A valid Shopify Admin API key must be provided'
    Default: 'your-shopify-admin-api-key-here'

Resources:
  MyShopifyAppEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: MyShopifyApp
      Tags:
        - Key: Application
          Value: ShopifyProductIntegration

  MyShopifyAppConnection:
    Type: AWS::Events::Connection
    Properties:
      Name: MyShopifyAppConnection
      Description: 'Connection for Shopify product creation API'
      AuthorizationType: API_KEY
      AuthParameters:
        ApiKeyAuthParameters:
          ApiKeyName: X-Shopify-Access-Token
          ApiKeyValue: !Ref ShopifyAdminApiKey

  MyShopifyAppApiDestination:
    Type: AWS::Events::ApiDestination
    Properties:
      Name: MyShopifyAppApiDestination
      ConnectionArn: !GetAtt MyShopifyAppConnection.Arn
      InvocationEndpoint: https://testanu.myshopify.com/admin/api/2023-04/products.json
      HttpMethod: POST

  EventBridgeToApiDestinationRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: EventBridgeApiDestinationAccess
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - events:InvokeApiDestination
                  - events:ListApiDestinations
                Resource: !GetAtt MyShopifyAppApiDestination.Arn

  EventBridgePutEventsRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: EventBridgePutEventsPermission
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - events:PutEvents
                Resource: !GetAtt MyShopifyAppEventBus.Arn

Outputs:
  EventBusName:
    Description: 'Name of the EventBridge event bus'
    Value: !Ref MyShopifyAppEventBus
  ApiDestinationName:
    Description: 'Name of the EventBridge API destination'
    Value: !Ref MyShopifyAppApiDestination
  ConnectionName:
    Description: 'Name of the EventBridge connection'
    Value: !Ref MyShopifyAppConnection

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'CloudFormation template to create EventBridge integration with Stripe Products API'

Metadata:
  AWS::CloudFormation::Interface:
    ParameterGroups:
      - Label:
          default: 'Stripe Configuration'
        Parameters:
          - StripeApiKey
    ParameterLabels:
      StripeApiKey:
        default: 'Stripe API Key'

Parameters:
  StripeApiKey:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Stripe API key for Authorization header (use SecureString parameter in production)'
    NoEcho: true
    Default: 'CHANGE_ME'

Resources:
  PartnerAppEventBus:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-PartnerAppEventBus'

  PartnerAppEventPattern:
    Type: AWS::Events::EventBus
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-PartnerAppPatternBus'

  PartnerAppConnection:
    Type: AWS::Events::Connection
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-StripeConnection'
      AuthorizationType: API_KEY
      AuthParameters:
        ApiKeyAuthParameters:
          ApiKeyName: Authorization
          ApiKeyValue: !Ref StripeApiKey
      Description: !Sub 'Connection to Stripe API for product creation from ${AWS::StackName}'

  PartnerApiDestination:
    Type: AWS::Events::ApiDestination
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-StripeProductsDestination'
      ConnectionArn: !GetAtt PartnerAppConnection.Arn
      InvocationEndpoint: https://api.stripe.com/v1/products
      InvocationRateLimitPerSecond: 10
      HttpMethod: POST

  EventBridgeInvokeRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-EventBridgeInvokeRole'
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: events.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-InvokeStripePolicy'
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - events:PutEvents
                  - events:InvokeApiDestination
                Resource:
                  - !GetAtt PartnerAppEventBus.Arn
                  - !GetAtt PartnerApiDestination.Arn
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackName
        - Key: StackName
          Value: !Ref AWS::StackName

Outputs:
  EventBusArn:
    Description: 'ARN of the EventBridge Event Bus'
    Value: !GetAtt PartnerAppEventBus.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-EventBusArn'

  ConnectionArn:
    Description: 'ARN of the EventBridge Connection'
    Value: !GetAtt PartnerAppConnection.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-ConnectionArn'

  ApiDestinationArn:
    Description: 'ARN of the EventBridge API Destination'
    Value: !GetAtt PartnerApiDestination.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-ApiDestinationArn'

  InvokeRoleArn:
    Description: 'ARN of the IAM Role for EventBridge to invoke API destination'
    Value: !GetAtt EventBridgeInvokeRole.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-InvokeRoleArn'
